mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cut periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.4pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces inflationary pressures, supporting corporate margins and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during tightening periods, invalidating the margin-support mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers immediate market repricing of inflation expectations, prompting anticipated central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a mean forward return below zero during confirmed expansion phases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly increases corporate earnings forecasts, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods yield negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the correlation between GDP growth and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that force corporate cost-cutting and trigger immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, refuting the bottleneck mechanism.
